threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver doesn’t stop at the ‘real’ bus stops. A true local knows to just wave them down.
Last night on the 63, a guy serenaded us with a full-on rendition of 'Wonderwall'. Everyone joined in by the time we hit the market. Classic Peckham vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last week, I saw a guy feeding pigeons while his toddler tried to catch them. That's when it hit me—this place is a circus, and I'm front row.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you sip a Berliner Weisse while listening to spontaneous poetry on the street. It's like a living art installation.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can name the exact time the BART delays start and end, like it’s part of their morning routine.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, a guy in a chicken suit tried to board the bus at Civic Center, the driver just laughed and told him to take the next one instead.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about Gaudí; they miss the local corners like Plaça de la Virreina, where real life unfolds over café con leche.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still call it "La Rambla" without the "de Catalunya" and look confused trying to find the right queue for 'xurros' at breakfast.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it 'the tube' instead of 'the underground' and keep looking for 'central' signs on the Overground. Classic rookie mistake!
Heard a guy on the N36 swear he saw a raccoon near the pub by the park. Sketchy, but it explains the bins being wrecked last weekend.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a hipster with a pet chicken casually crossing Karl-Marx-Straße. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a club in an old power plant where the morning light streams through broken windows as ravers keep dancing with the sun.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they still call it 'Civic Center' instead of 'Twitter Park' and remember when the plaza had a fountain.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo pull out a saxophone and play ‘Take Five’ on the 6. Muni’s unpredictability showcases our city’s quirky heart.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we're all about Gaudí, but the real vibe is in the local plazas where families chat and kids play – that’s our heart.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still ask locals for directions to Parc Güell—no one from here goes that route anymore.
